Text: This military leader rose to power during the French Revolution and became First Consul in 1799. ||| He established the Napoleonic Code, which still influences legal systems today. ||| His disastrous invasion of Russia in 1812 marked the beginning of his downfall. ||| He was finally defeated at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815.

This American author wrote about a mysterious millionaire who throws lavish parties on Long Island. ||| The novel features the narrator Nick Carraway and is set during the Jazz Age. ||| Its famous last line mentions being 'borne back ceaselessly into the past.' ||| Published in 1925, this work by F. Scott Fitzgerald has become a classic of American literature.

This organelle is often called the powerhouse of the cell. ||| It has its own DNA separate from the cell's nuclear DNA. ||| Its inner membrane is folded into structures called cristae. ||| It produces ATP through the process of oxidative phosphorylation.

This composer's Symphony No. 9 includes the 'Ode to Joy' in its final movement. ||| Born in Bonn in 1770, he moved to Vienna where he studied with Haydn. ||| He continued composing even after becoming completely deaf. ||| His works bridge the Classical and Romantic periods of music.

This ancient wonder of the world was located in Alexandria, Egypt. ||| Standing approximately 100 meters tall, it guided ships into the harbor. ||| Built during the reign of Ptolemy II, it was one of the tallest man-made structures for centuries. ||| It was destroyed by earthquakes between the 12th and 15th centuries.

This programming language was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. ||| It emphasizes code readability with its use of significant whitespace. ||| Its philosophy is summarized in 'The Zen of' this language, accessible via an Easter egg. ||| Popular in data science and machine learning, it's named after a British comedy troupe.

This Shakespearean tragedy features a Danish prince who struggles with revenge. ||| The title character delivers the famous 'To be or not to be' soliloquy. ||| Other characters include Ophelia, Polonius, and Claudius. ||| The play ends with multiple deaths including the protagonist's.

This economic theory advocates for minimal government intervention in markets. ||| Developed by Adam Smith in 'The Wealth of Nations,' it describes an 'invisible hand' guiding the economy. ||| It suggests that individual self-interest can lead to collective benefit. ||| This approach contrasts with Keynesian economics.

This planet is known as the Red Planet due to iron oxide on its surface. ||| It has two small moons named Phobos and Deimos. ||| A day on this planet lasts approximately 24 hours and 37 minutes. ||| NASA's Perseverance rover is currently exploring its surface.

This artistic movement began in Paris in the 1860s. ||| Artists like Monet and Renoir focused on capturing light and its changing qualities. ||| The name comes from Monet's painting 'Impression, Sunrise.' ||| This style emphasized painting outdoors and visible brush strokes.
